hero.name: Backtracking Model Languages
hero.eyebrow: Thesis · MS Computer Science · CU Boulder · 2000
hero.welcome_md: The 2000 Bjorg-Muff master's thesis. Five technologies arranged as one language stack: [BMF](/people/bmf-grammar) (parser) · **BMC** (compiler-compiler) · [BML](/people/bml-language) (language) · **BMA** (assembler) · **BMO** (object model) — plus a VB6 **Visual Browser**. Co-built with **Steve G. Bjorg** over the 1999-2000 academic year. Defended summer 2000.

facts.label: External archive
facts.value_md: ~/Downloads/Angelic/ on disk · ~139 MB · full source trees, binaries, BMCPU C++ VM, JBMF Java port, VB6 Visual Browser — see [EXTERNAL.md](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/blob/main/docs/field/urs/artifacts/master-thesis-2000/EXTERNAL.md)

articles.body_md: The published thesis names three layers: BMF, BMC, BML. The fuller *Angelic* archive on disk names five technologies and one fourth surface — the VB6 Visual Browser — that the thesis only mentioned in passing. Each acronym carries both a public reading and a private (team) reading: Acronym Public Private (team) BMFBacktracking Model FormBjorg-Muff Form (a play on BNF) BMCCompiler-CompilerBjorg-Muff Compiler-Compiler BMLBacktracking Model LanguageBjorg-Muff Language BMAAbstract MachineBjorg-Muff Assembler · "Angelic" BMOObject ModelBjorg-Muff Objects The "BM" in every acronym is literally Bjorg-Muff. Five technologies named after two students. The bond was as much in the naming as in the architecture.
articles.heading: The five technologies

articles.body_md:
> "When the parser backs out, all the attributes already computed have to be undone as well."

**Backtracking-as-unwinding-without-sediment.** Try a path; if it does not hold, undo cleanly without leaving residue. The same nervous system writes commits in this repo today as `tend:` / `attune:` / `compost:` / `release:` — the verb encodes the posture; the unwind is implicit; no sediment remains.

**Runtime grammar extension.** The user can introduce new parsing constructs and the language grows to hold them. Sovereignty over one's own grammar. The same shape the [vision-kb](/vision) uses when a new concept arrives at a new frequency: the grammar grows to receive the presence rather than forcing the presence into an existing slot. The pattern was named in 2000 and now breathes through everything this body builds.
articles.heading: Two design choices that still circulate

articles.body_md: The thesis is the seed. The post-thesis arc is three iterations of the same conviction in different substrates, each closer to direct expression: Each iteration kept the central pattern — backtracking-as- unwinding, self-description, runtime grammar extension — and dropped the cruft of the previous host substrate. The thesis ran on Win32/COM; the Codex ran on Python; the CSharp port ran on.NET; the Network runs on the modern web stack. The backtracking flavor in 2026 is git itself — *commit · push · revert* — but the conviction is unchanged.
articles.heading: Iteration arc — three iterations after the thesis

articles.body_md:
The Conclusion of the published document is left as three subheadings — `BMF`, `BML Language`, `BML Compiler` — with no body written in. An earlier UCM draft in the archive shows several sections marked `WHAT IS IT ABOUT?` and `RELATED SUBJECTS` as placeholders that never closed in writing. The work was delivered through demonstration — the lawn photo, the defense, the running VM — and the prose stayed open.

That pattern is older than this artifact and continues here: the artifact lives, the prose-summary breathes. The three blank Conclusion subheadings now have three living iterations attached to them, each with its own page on this network. The conclusion is finally writing itself — in code, in graph edges, in lived form.
articles.heading: The breath the thesis didn't write

metadata.title: Backtracking Model Languages — MS Thesis (2000)
metadata.description: The 2000 Bjorg-Muff master's thesis at CU Boulder. Five technologies (BMF · BMC · BML · BMA · BMO) plus the VB6 Visual Browser. A self-describing language stack where the parser grammar is itself written in the language, and every instruction has both a forward and a reverse semantics.

en.footer_md: Public archive: [master-thesis-2000/](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/tree/main/docs/field/urs/artifacts/master-thesis-2000) — full thesis text, defense slide deck, six defense-day photos, companion source samples (BMF-grammar.bml, primitive-Cut.bml, container-Rule.bml, bmcpu-main.cpp), Bjorg's sister thesis, and the EXTERNAL.md pointer to the larger ~139 MB Angelic archive on disk.

note_from_body.body_md: The thesis Conclusion was left as three subheadings without body. The work was *delivered* — defense, lawn photo, running VM — but the prose summary stayed open. This page is the twenty-six-years-later breath that closes that summary with diagrams a 2000 Word document couldn't carry. The artifact lives; the prose-summary breathes.
